Name: Het Regie Collectief
Description:
Wij regisseren complexe huisvestingsprojecten met de juiste mensen, heldere processen en een gedeeld doel. Voor solide resultaten die staan, van eerste schets tot realisatie.

Vraagstukken rondom huisvesting vinden wij prachtig. Er komt zoveel samen: de wereld vraagt erom, het is buitengewoon complex en nauwkeurigheid is essentieel.
Maar minstens zo belangrijk: wij vinden dit het allermooiste wat er is. De complexe keten van samenwerkingen, met alle betrokken partijen en belangen, werkt alleen als regie een rol van betekenis pakt.

Regie betekent in onze definitie: gelijkwaardigheid van partners, duidelijke afstemming, flexibel samenwerken en een helder gezamenlijk doel:optimaal gebruiksresultaat voor de eindgebruikers. Wij zijn op onze plek als het belang van zowel de huisvestiging als de regie evident is. Dan laten we onze ervaring gelden met een toon die ongeëvenaard is, in haar combinatie van denken en doen.

Name: Move Forward MN
Description: Founded in 1990, Move Forward is a nonprofit organization providing housing case management and mental health therapy services to youth ages 11-24 in the west metro. Homelessness prevention programs include case management, drop in and outreach services, schools to housing, and a scattered site transitional living program. Mental health services are school and community based, increasing access to quality mental health care and reducing barriers related to finances, childcare, limited social support, transportation, and stigma. The majority of participants identify with BIPOC and/or LGBTQIA+ communities and are often uninsured or underinsured. Move Forward promotes trauma-informed, person-centered, and strengths-based approaches.

Name: Central Vermont Habitat for Humanity, Inc.
Description: Central Vermont Habitat for Humanity, LLC is an affordable housing non-profit. We build and rehabilitate housing and offer affordable, zero or low interest, mortgages for income-sensitive Vermonters. We are located in Vermont's state capital, Montpelier, and serve Washington and Orange Counties.

Name: Habitat for Humanity Maui
Description:
Mission: To build decent housing and to renovate substandard housing on Maui in partnership with community volunteers and potential homeowners so that homelessness and substandard housing on Maui is eliminated.

Habitat for Humanity Maui builds and sells homes to underserved families on zero interest mortgages and no-profit basis. We serve families earning 25-80% of Maui County's median income. Habitat for Humanity Maui believes that through shelter we can build strength and stability for families and our community.

All are welcome! Habitat for Humanity Maui has an open-door policy and believe that everyone deserves a decent, affordable place to live regardless of race, color, religion, gender, national origin/ancestry, familial status, disability, marital status, age, sexual orientation, political views or any other distinctions that too often divide people. We welcome everyone from all backgrounds.

Name: Utah Non Profit Housing Corporation
Description:
Founded in 1967, Utah Non-Profit Housing Corporation (UNPHC) is Utah’s largest non-profit developer of affordable multi-family housing. We are a 501(c)3 organization and a Community Housing Development Organization (CHDO). UNPHC has developed or acquired/rehabilitated more than 50 Family, Senior and Special Needs Properties to date, which serves in excess of 2500 households, the majority of whom make less than 20% of the area median income. More than 18,000 households have been part of the Utah Non-Profit Housing family.

The mission of Utah Nonprofit Housing Corporation (UNPHC) is to improve “quality of life” by providing decent, safe, affordable housing, focusing on low- and very-low-income individuals and families.

Name: Hope Housing
Description: Hope Housing is a Bradford based charity providing safe supported accommodation and personalised services to help people move out of homelessness and rebuild their lives.

Name: Peabody Properties
Description: JFS Secured Staffing provides staffing services to the nursing and manufacturing industries. They assist with worker's compensation, liabilities, payroll, background checks, interviews, and filing of employee information, saving companies time and money. Specializing in nursing, they offer staffing for HHA, CNA, LPN, and RN positions. In the manufacturing sector, they handle high-volume staffing needs, ensuring employee safety certifications, workers compensation, and background checks. With a focus on quick response and user-friendly processes, JFS Secured Staffing aims to meet staffing requirements efficiently and effectively.

Name: HousingNet-AU
Description: HousingNet platform application connects NDIS participants need the perfect home in SIL, SDA, Accommodation Provider and Allied Health provider services Australia wide.

Name: Newton Housing Authority
Description:
The Newton Housing Authority was created in 1959 and is the largest provider of affordable housing in the City of Newton. The NHA provides housing assistance to over 1000 low-income residents through the administration of federal and state-sponsored subsidized housing programs and the Section 8 Housing Choice Voucher Program. The NHA also owns and operates a management portfolio of 93 studio, one, two, and three-bedroom units.

The NHA has a responsibility to provide and maintain a high standard of housing, which allows our residents a safe, clean, and affordable home in which to reside. The NHA also endeavors to create a sense of community for its residents through its social service programs, so as to enhance their quality of life within an environment of dignity and caring.

In recent years, the NHA has worked to create more affordable housing in the City of Newton and greatly expand our Resident Services Program, providing enhanced tenant-focused social services and clinical case management to better meet the changing needs of our residents.

Name: Tucson Recovery Homes
Description: Tucson Recovery Homes is committed to offering a compassionate and structured living space for those in recovery. We strive to create a nurturing environment that promotes emotional, physical, and spiritual well-being. Through peer support, structured living, and dedicated guidance, our mission is to help each person build the resilience, strength, and skills necessary to maintain a lasting, healthy recovery.

Name: Housing Authority City Of Prichard
Description:
History
The Housing Authority of the City of Prichard, Alabama (HACP) came into being under the provisions of the Housing Authorities Act No. 56, approved February 8, 1935 and Act No. 148, approved March 17, 1939, now termed the Code of Alabama of 1940.

Name: Crossroads Housing and Support
Description:
We are an organisation working with Birmingham city council to combat homelessness within the city. 

Our goal is to become the benchmark of supported housing.

We provide safe, clean and affordable rooms in shared houses, combined with weekly support to help our residents back into work, education and independence.

Our service caters to males and females, 21 years of age and over. 

Individuals who are on the verge of homelessness but with the right space and support will see themselves back on a positive course.

Name: Tallahassee Lenders' Consortium
Description: As a NeighborWorks Home Ownership Center our goal is to increase home ownership and revitalize neighborhoods.  TLC provides information and education regarding credit counseling and the process of purchasing a home to low income and very low income families. In addition, TLC administers the City of Tallahassee and Leon County's Down Payment Assistance Programs and assists local lenders in making affordable loans by processing, underwriting, and packaging these loans.  Through the collaboration of residents and business leaders, government and nonprofit partners, the NeighborWorks Home Ownership Center offers all of the services and training needed to shop for, purchase, rehabilitate, insure, maintain, and manage a home.

Name: Housing Works (Central Oregon Regional Housing Authority)
Description:
At Housing Works, our mission is simple: Fostering Dignity Through Housing. We believe dignity starts with quality, affordable housing. Serving Deschutes, Crook, and Jefferson counties in Central Oregon, we offer affordable homes and services to help individuals build a better future.

We proudly administer a federal contract with the Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) to provide rental assistance through the Housing Choice Voucher (HCV) program. Currently, we assist over 1,400 families and individuals monthly, bringing more than $9 million annually into the Central Oregon rental market. The program involves over 500 participating local landlords, strengthening the community’s housing infrastructure.

We provide homes for workforce, seniors, veterans, and more. From affordable housing to savings accounts, credit-building loans, and home ownership support, we offer essential services that help individuals thrive.

We are committed to creating an environment where every team member has ongoing access to opportunities. Our culture is built on teamwork, collaboration, open communication, transparency, and ongoing staff training.

At Housing Works, we understand that a home is more than just a place to live; it is the foundation for stability, growth, and opportunity. Through our work, we are not only providing housing but creating lasting change in the lives of individuals and families across Central Oregon. Our commitment to fostering dignity through housing extends beyond just our clients to our staff, partners, and the community. Together, we are building a future where everyone has access to the resources and support they need to thrive. Join us in shaping a more prosperous tomorrow, one home at a time.

Name: Hope Foundation for Foster Youth Housing
Description:
The Hope Foundation for Foster Youth Housing is a nonprofit organization dedicated to providing eligible former foster children with the financial resources necessary to secure safe and stable housing as they transition into adulthood. We believe that every young person deserves a safe and stable place to call home, and we are committed to helping former foster youth achieve their goals and reach their full potential.
Our Mission
The mission of the Hope Foundation For Foster Youth Housing (HFFYH) is to provide eligible former foster children with the financial resources necessary to secure safe and stable housing as they transition into adulthood. Through the use of Title IV-E funds, the HFFYH  aims to prevent homelessness among former foster youth and provide them with a foundation for success.
Our Vision
Our vision is to create a world where all former foster youth have access to safe and stable housing as they transition into adulthood. We envision a future where all youth has the opportunity to build stable and fulfilling lives, free from the negative impacts of homelessness and instability.
COMPANY CORE VALUES
Integrity
 We uphold the highest ethical standards in all our actions, ensuring transparency and trustworthiness in our work
People
 We prioritize the well-being and empowerment of every individual we serve, ensuring their voices are heard, and their needs are met.
Compassion
We approach our work with empathy and understanding, creating a safe and supportive environment where youth can thrive
Fairness
 We believe in treating everyone with fairness and justice, ensuring equal opportunities for all youth in accessing housing and support services.
Equity
 We strive for equity by addressing systemic barriers and providing resources that promote equal opportunities for all young people
Problem-solving
 We are dedicated to finding innovative solutions to the challenges faced by homeless youth.
Hope
We foster a sense of optimism, belief in brighter future.

Name: Excel Development
Description:
Excel houses approximately 900 individuals and families with Severe and Persistent Mental Illness (SPMI), most of whom were homeless. We leverage our property portfolio and partner with private landlords throughout Hamilton County to meet each participant’s unique housing needs.

If you or someone you know is experiencing homelessness and needs shelter or housing, please call the Central Access Point line at (513)381-SAFE.

If you or someone you know needs Mental Health resources, please call the Mental Health Access Point at (513)558-8888

Name: Hudson County Housing Resource Center
Description:
Hudson County Housing Resource Center, Inc. is a non-profit organization located at 830 Bergen Ave, Jersey City, New Jersey, United States.
The mission of Hudson County Housing Resource Center (HRC) is to prevent homelessness through direct services. HRC offers free and confidential HUD Approved Rental Housing Counseling, Tenant Based Rental Assistance to disabled and low income Hudson County residents, as well as Project Based Affordable Housing to low income families.

Name: EDIL MEGA APPALTI SRL
Description:
Edil Mega Appalti S.r.l. è un'impresa che opera nel settore dell’edilizia, rappresentando un simbolo di qualità, professionalità e innovazione. Fondata da Enrico Cinti, si è affermata nel mercato per l'eccellenza dei suoi servizi e per l'impegno continuo verso il miglioramento.
Con una visione chiara e un approccio orientato al cliente, l'azienda si impegna a soddisfare le esigenze specifiche di ogni progetto, offrendo soluzioni su misura che combinano tecnologia avanzata e competenza artigianale. 

Servizi Offerti
Offriamo una vasta gamma di servizi, tra cui:

Ristrutturazione interi edifici: rifacimento facciate, tetti, balconi e interni.

Interventi per condomini, privati e attività commerciali: lavori di ristrutturazione, manutenzione e riqualificazione.

Progetti conto terzi: lavori per enti pubblici o altre aziende. Ogni intervento è personalizzato in base alle necessità del cliente.

Qualità e Sicurezza
Certificati ISO 9001:2015 e SOA OG1 Classifica II, garantiamo lavori conformi alle normative di sicurezza e qualità. Ogni progetto è seguito con cura dalla Titolare e dal nostro team di tecnici esperti, assicurando efficienza e affidabilità in ogni fase.

Dove Operiamo
Lavoriamo principalmente a Roma e Milano, con due sedi operative nelle rispettive province, ma siamo in grado di gestire interventi anche in altre aree.

Contatti
Sede legale: Via Licinio Bernardini 1, Palestrina (RM)
Sede operativa Roma: Via Colle Silvano 33, Palestrina (RM)
Sede operativa Milano: Via Ponchielli Amilcare 8, Milano (MI)
Telefono: 0687083915 / 3286855502
Email: edilmegaappalti@gmail.com 
Email: info@edilmegaappalti.it

Name: Genbro
Description:
Hos GenBro ved vi, at mange ældre lever med ønsket om et rigere socialt liv, og vi ved også, at der er stor mangel på økonomisk overkommelige studieboliger. GenBros formål er derfor at tilbyde en unik løsning, hvor vi gennem vores service matcher ældre, der kan tilbyde et værelse til udlejning, med unge, som vil engagere sig socialt i en ny kollektiv boform. 

Hos GenBro bygger vi bro mellem generationer, fordi vi tror på, at der er meget at vinde for alle parter; ikke mindst i forhold til mentalt velbefindende og erfaringsudveksling.

Vi matcher vores ansøgere og sørger for at indlede kontakten. En del af vores service inkluderer løbende opfølgning, så vi kan sikre os, at udlejningen forløber succesfuldt. 

Indtil videre er GenBro en startup i den indledende fase. Følg udviklingen her på siden eller på vores Facebook eller Instagram.

Name: SOS HOUSING
Description: My career as a real estate attorney led me to co-found a nonprofit organization in 2010, opening, managing and operating shared housing models, including sober living homes and senior & student housing, which allowed me to help mentor other group home owners in their business. I dedicated 15 years to housing more than 30000 people, most coming home from the justice system. It is time to transition into my passion of teaching others and providing consultation, coaching and speaking services for all shared housing businesses. My extensive knowledge and experience with Group Homes is available for those who want to make a social impact in their communities by providing affordable housing and existing real estate investors wanting to increase and double their rental income. With the nationwide shortage of affordable housing and rising housing costs, shared living is poised to become a moving force in the real estate market.  I am available for consultations, coaching, and speaking at events on cohousing, coliving, and shared housing real estate. I teach the ins and outs of running businesses such as sober living homes, transitional housing, student housing, senior housing, reentry housing, and much more. Housing is Not a Privilege by a Human Right!

Name: Housing Development Corporation
Description:
Housing Development Corporation (HDC) is a nonprofit organization dedicated to expanding affordable housing opportunities and improving the quality of life for individuals and families across Central Nebraska.

We serve residents in Hall, Adams, Clay, Nuckolls, and Webster counties through a range of housing solutions tailored to meet community needs—from lease-to-own programs and affordable rentals to down payment assistance and owner-occupied home repair.

As rising costs threaten housing stability, HDC works to bridge the gap by developing strategic partnerships, providing financial education, and advocating for long-term, sustainable housing development.

Our mission is rooted in the belief that everyone deserves a safe, affordable place to call home.

Key Programs:

Owner-Occupied Repair
Affordable Rentals & Lease-to-Own Housing
Down Payment Assistance
Financial Literacy & Homebuyer Education (RentWise & REACH)

Name: Sunrise Foundation (AU)
Description:
Sunrise Foundation is a registered charity committed to tackling homelessness and improving housing accessibility across Australia. We acquire and develop properties in both metropolitan and regional areas, ensuring they are available for rent or purchase by individuals who are vulnerable or disadvantaged.

Our Vision

We strive to foster social inclusion, equal opportunity, and dignity by providing safe, high-quality accommodation. Our goal is to enhance the well-being of those in our community who face significant challenges and barriers to securing stable housing.

Let’s get chatting!

Name: The Housing Authority of the City of Hoboken
Description: The Hoboken Housing Authority (HHA) is the largest provider of very low and low-income housing in Hoboken. Established in 1949, HHA provides access to decent and safe affordable housing low income families, the elderly, and persons with disabilities. The HHA five affordable housing developments serve 1354 households and approximately 5370 residents. HHA meets the growing needs of our local communities by administering up to 225 Housing Choice Vouchers (Section 8) within the city limits. In addition, HHA connects residents to opportunities and community services to improve their quality of life and help them become self-sufficient.

Name: Acharya Jagadish Chandra Bose Hall Of Residence, IIT Kharagpur
Description: Acharya Jagdish Chandra Bose Hall of Residence, also known as JCB Hall is one of the student halls of residence for male undergraduate and postgraduate students. Acharya J. C. Bose Hall of Residence was established in 1962.This Hall was named after Acharya Jagadish Chandra Bose, a polymath, a physicist, biologist, botanist, archaeologist, and writer of science fiction. The hall has been divided in 6 blocks namely A, B, C East, C West, E and F block. Each of the blocks has one floor above the ground floor.

Name: Federal Practice Group, LLC
Description: Federal Practice Group (FPG) uses its expertise in affordable housing to help government managers create and implement successful programs. These programs are crucial to the lives of the most vulnerable Americans; we care deeply about maximizing each program's impact while also being a good steward of taxpayer dollars. We enjoy getting to work with a variety of people at all levels of an agency, alongside leadership, management, staff, and local developers.

Name: Vermont State Housing Authority
Description: The Vermont State Housing Authority's Mission is to promote and expand the supply of affordable rental and homeownership opportunities on a statewide basis. Each new endeavor will enhance or increase the organization's capacity to continue its mission and to assure the effectiveness of VSHA as a provider and administrator of affordable housing programs.

Name: Lily Services
Description:
Lily Services is formed by 3 companies:
The Lily Housing Project
Lily Residential Services
Lily Staffing Solutions

The Lily Housing Project
We provide our young residents with a space to grow with confidence, enhance their self-esteem and develop the life skills required to live independently. We operate 6 supported housing projects across our organisation 4 in Weymouth, 1 in Dorchester and 1 in Ferndown. We support young people aged 16 - 21 years old. 

Utilising our strong understanding, experience and total commitment to delivering positive outcomes, we have offered specialised provisions for high need, high level vulnerability and high-risk young people for nearly 20 years. Keeping our residents safe whilst building towards a planned move-on into independent living through engagement, participation and clear communication with key stakeholders are crucial building blocks within our approach and organisation.

Lily Residential Services
Lily Residential Services are very proud to have 4 registered homes in Dorset. We are have 3 homes in Weymouth and 1 in Poole. Our homes are relatively small provisions, able to house and support two children each, aged 9-17 years. Who have complex emotional, social and behavioural needs, 365 days a year.

We provide full-time staff with specialist skills to meet the individual needs of the children in our care.  With a nurturing and person-centred approach, we enable children with the ability to continue learning and developing life skills 

Lily Staffing Solutions
Lily Staffing Solutions is a community-based recruitment service that works with local authorities and other care providers within Children and young people’s services in Dorset.

We are part of the Lily group who have historically provided safe environments and care for vulnerable children and young people in Dorset, consistently demonstrating positive outcomes. Through our 20 years of experience, we know how important it is to find the right staff.

Name: Christian Care QC
Description: Christian Care is a holistic faith based homeless service provider offering emergency shelter & transitional housing, supportive services, street outreach, and a community meal site. Grounded in core values of faith, hope, and charity with a mission of empowering individuals to make positive changes in their lives.

Name: Obedi Home
Description: Welcome to Obedi Home, an all-male adult foster care home providing 24/7 care for individuals with behavioral health needs. We create a structured, compassionate environment where residents feel safe, respected, and supported in their daily lives.

Name: Peel Cheshire Homes (Brampton) Inc
Description:
Peel Cheshire Homes Brampton owns and operates a twelve (12) unit Supportive Housing home which provides residents a private room with a bathroom, attendant care, food preparation, and other services which support independent living. 
The agency also provides an Outreach Program for physically disabled adults who live in their own homes within the city of Brampton. PSW's assist the "consumer" with daily needs such as bathing, meal prep, grocery shopping, etc.

Name: NavajoHousingAuthority
Description: Welcome to the Navajo Housing Authority. Leaders in housing our nation through community partnerships.

Name: Genossenschaft WeitWohnen Solothurn
Description:
Vision / Grundidee 
Unser Projekt will Jung und Alt, Einzelpersonen, Familien und Gruppen ein attraktives Zuhause in einer nachhaltigen, generationengemischten Siedlung anbieten. Wir wollen Menschen unterschiedlicher Herkunft mit Interesse an gemeinschaftlichem Leben zusammenbringen und ihnen Wohnungen, Gemeinschaftsräume und Arbeitsplätze anbieten. 
Unser autofreies Konzept orientiert sich am Modell der "2000-Watt-Gesellschaft" (dies ist ein energiepolitisches Modell, das im Rahmen des Programms Novatlantis an der Eidgenössischen Technischen Hochschule Zürich (ETHZ) entwickelt wurde. Gemäß dieser Vision sollte der Energiebedarf jedes Erdenbewohners einer durchschnittlichen Leistung von 2000 Watt auf Stufe Primärenergie entsprechen) . Wir wollen einen sorgsamen Umgang mit unseren Ressourcen im Sinne der Kreislaufwirtschaft pflegen: Was wenig bringt und die Kosten verteuert, wird weggelassen. Durch die Reduktion auf das Wesentliche wird der Blick frei für Werte, die in unserer gewinnorientierten Zeit immer stärker in den Hintergrund gerückt sind: Soziale Geborgenheit, Solidarität, Mitwirkung.

Ci occupiamo principalmente di edifici pubblici, infrastrutture, edifici privati grazie al lavoro di manodopera qualificata e specializzata direttamente alle nostre dipendenze, supportata dal nostro ufficio tecnico.

A partire dal 2016 operiamo anche applicando tecniche costruttive a secco, prendendoci carico anche delle finiture di interni.

Attualmente l’organico è composto da circa 40 persone fra carpentieri, muratori, manovali, ponteggisti, maestranze tecniche e direttive. Tale organico, a seconda delle richieste dei committenti e della mole di lavoro che viene acquisito, può essere implementato per far fronte anche a cantieri di grandi dimensioni.

L’impresa BRB Costruzioni Edili Srl si avvale inoltre di studi professionali fidelizzali esterni per la gestione dei cantieri in materia di sicurezza e medicina del lavoro, i quali provvedono alla redazione di tutta la documentazione tecnico/amministrativa prevista dalle vigenti norme in materia di cantieristica edile e normalmente richiesta dai nostri clienti prima dell’inizio dei lavori affidati. Si occupano inoltre di mantenere costantemente monitorato e aggiornato sia l’aspetto sanitario dei dipendenti (visite mediche, controlli sanitari, etc.), sia quello relativo agli attestati professionali di formazione dei singoli addetti (gruisti, ponteggisti, preposti di cantiere, addetti alle emergenze, RLS, corsi di base, aggiornamenti periodici, etc.) al fine di operare nel rispetto della legge.

Name: Project America, Inc.
Description:
Project America, Inc. is a 501(c)3 nonprofit transforming our nation one life at a time.  We provide level 2 shared housing for those overcoming Substance Use Disorder (SUD).  We use the proven approach  laid out by Kate’s House Foundation.  Our houses provide safe homes where program members become connected to a family house.  The peer model of recovery increases the likelihood of success and greatly reduces recidivism.  

While we can calculate the financial return a shared housing bed returns to society, the realized value of our program cannot be fully calculated in dollars.  How much is it worth to a family to have a father, mother, son, or daughter restored?  Substance use disorder and justice issues have tremendous negative emotional and financial tolls on the family.   Successful recovery has a positive impact on multiple generations.  It breaks the downward spiral caused by addiction and replaces it with a vibrant legacy.  You can become a partner today in transforming lives.

Project America is not affiliated with any organization.  It is faith-inspired rather than faith-based.  People of all faiths are welcome.  The key requirement is the strong desire and commitment to make a positive change to overcome substance use disorder or justice issues.  Today is the best day to start.  See how you can become a member of our program.

Name: Revolving Doors Housing Trust
Description:
Our Vision

To make a positive and real difference to the lives of the people who use our service during their journey of change and to offer a bespoke service that transforms lives.

Our Mission

To participate in reduction and eradication of homelessness within Greater Manchester and the Northwest of England in general. Through cooperatively working with Local Authorities and other interested organisations.
To provide a haven for vulnerable adults and enabling them to live a fulfilling live.

Our Values

We believe Everyone Deserves another Chance!

Our Staff

We have a team of support workers who work tirelessly for our residents. Residents are also supported by, a registered Mental Health Nurse, a Drug and Alcohol Support worker, a safeguarding lead, and a dedicated Resettlement Officer. In addition to outside agencies and volunteers.

This TRIAGE support system was established to offer a “One-Stop Shop” for residents helping to reduce the stress and anxiety associated with trying to sort things out alone. Service users in our hostels are assessed, supported, and appointed a key worker within 7 days of being accommodated, they are also assessed and given information and referrals to external agencies to speed up their journey of change.


Our Services

We pledge to offer the following to our residents:

•	Safe and secure accommodation with appropriate support to prevent further homelessness.
•	Practical support to tackle issues relating to ill health, welfare rights, education, employment, access to vital services, and other issues that interfere with a residents’ quality of life.
•	Protection from abuse, aggressive behaviour, and violence by adopting a      zero-tolerance policy towards crime and anti-social behaviour.
•	Assistance with securing suitable and appropriate permanent accommodation, in both the public and private housing sector.

Name: MedSurf Co-Living Solutions
Description:
MedSurf is a national housing network supporting the next generation of doctors and healthcare learners.
We connect vetted medical students and trainees with furnished, affordable housing during clinical rotations—solving a quiet crisis in medical education that too often goes ignored.

What started as a mission to help one student avoid sleeping in her car has grown into a scalable infrastructure solution rooted in trust, community, and equity.

We work with homeowners, landlords, and mission-driven hosts across the country to turn extra rooms into safe, supportive homes for the healthcare workforce of tomorrow. In return, hosts receive reliable income and the satisfaction of knowing they’re making a lasting impact.

For hospitals, universities, and health systems, MedSurf offers peace of mind:
 Your learners are housed safely
 Burnout, dropout, and equity gaps are reduced
 Your reputation for supporting students is elevated

 What We Offer
Vetted housing for medical students, residents, and allied health learners
Host onboarding + readiness support
Customized solutions for universities, nonprofits, and hospital partners
Lease templates, setup guidance, and insurance resources
“Done-for-you” options for eligible properties

 Why It Matters
Clinical education is demanding enough. No student should be forced to choose between unsafe housing and their future.
MedSurf exists to create dignity, safety, and access—one home, one host, one rotation at a time.

Name: Charisma's Heart
Description:
Founded in 2024, Charisma’s Heart was envisioned as a housing-focused organization committed to helping women recover and transition to a thriving state by offering a safe and supportive environment that fosters healing, empowerment, and community.

Our mission targets families experiencing financial strain due to low income, homelessness, or other economic hardships, compounded by the effects of domestic violence or trauma. Additionally, we support individuals who find themselves in a challenging position where their income is too high to qualify for typical government assistance programs, yet too low to comfortably afford essential needs. 

Our goal is to bridge this gap by offering affordable and accessible residential care options tailored to the unique needs of families facing financial challenges. We strive to provide a nurturing and supportive environment where women and children can access the care and assistance they need to thrive.

Name: Perpart
Description:
A Pernambuco Participações e Investimentos S/A (Perpart) é uma empresa estadual sob governança as Secretaria de Desenvolvimento Urbano e Habitação (Seduh). Criada pela Lei N.º 11.314, de 29 de dezembro de 1995, a Perpart teve inicialmente como finalidade a geração de recursos para alocação em investimentos públicos. Com alteração em seu objeto, firmado pela Lei N.º 11.671, de 27 de setembro de 1999, acumulou em suas atribuições a administração dos créditos, direitos e obrigações decorrentes da incorporação de outras empresas públicas e sociedades de economia mista de que o Estado detenha o controle acionário.

A Perpart é sucessora de sete empresas: a Companhia de Habitação Popular do Estado de Pernambuco (Cohab-PE), a Empresa de Assistência Técnica e Extensão Rural do Estado de Pernambuco (Emater-PE), a Empresa de Abastecimento e Extensão Rural do Estado de Pernambuco (Ebape); a Companhia Pernambucana do Meio Ambiente (CPRH), a Empresa de Fomento da Informática do Estado de Pernambuco (Fisepe); a Companhia de Abastecimento e Armazéns Gerais do Estado de Pernambuco (Ceagepe); e a Empresa Metropolitana de Transportes Urbanos (EMTU).

A Perpart, antes alocada na estrutura da Secretaria de Administração, passou a integrar a estrutura da Secretaria da Casa Civil, a partir da Lei n° 16.683, de 1º de novembro de 2019, e desde 18 de janeiro de 2023 é vinculada à Secretaria de Desenvolvimento Urbano e Habitação, como especifica a Lei nº 18.139.

A missão da empresa é gerir direitos e obrigações de empresas extintas e em extinção pelo Governo do Estado de Pernambuco, desenvolver ações inovadoras na área de gestão pública – possibilitando oportunidades de negócios – e promover a regularização fundiária e imobiliária para a sociedade.

Name: AISL - Association Insertion Solidarité Logement
Description:
Depuis près de 30 ans, l’AISL (Association pour l’Insertion par le Logement) œuvre pour permettre à des ménages en grande difficulté d’accéder à un logement adapté et d’y être accompagnés. Spécialisée dans l’accompagnement des publics en situation de précarité, l’association est un acteur clé sur le territoire nantais et son agglomération.

Nos valeurs : humanisme, solidarité, bienveillance, accès au logement pour tous et respect de la diversité.

Nos missions :
•	Rechercher et gérer des logements sociaux
•	Accompagner socialement les ménages hébergés
•	Assurer une gestion locative et technique adaptée
•	Favoriser l’accès aux droits et aux services sociaux

L’AISL met en place un accompagnement global et personnalisé, en mobilisant un réseau d’acteurs locaux pour garantir à chacun un parcours résidentiel sécurisé et digne.

Name: Cooper Charitable Foundation
Description:
Our mission is to improve people’s lives by helping them secure stable housing.

The Cooper Charitable Foundation was created in 2023 by John Cooper. Originally from Hampton, Virginia, John moved to Raleigh, North Carolina, in 1999 to attend North Carolina State University and study civil engineering.  While in college, he founded Cooper Tacia General Contracting Company, a firm that he grew from doing small bathroom and kitchen remodels with his own hands to a multimillion-dollar company executing commercial, municipal, higher education, airport and industrial construction work throughout the southeastern United States. John started The Cooper Charitable Foundation with funds generated from the success of his company.

Name: ARMOS-oi
Description:
Bienvenue sur la page LinkedIn de l’ARMOS Océan Indien !
Depuis 75 ans, les bailleurs sociaux de La Réunion construisent, rénovent et innovent pour répondre aux besoins des habitants. L’ARMOS-oi fédère aujourd’hui 7 acteurs majeurs du logement social : SEMADER, SEMAC, SHLMR, SODIAC, SEDRE, SIDR et SODEGIS.
Notre action s’articule autour de 4 grandes missions : - Informer en diffusant des données fiables sur le logement social- Créer du lien entre bailleurs et partenaires- Valoriser l’impact économique, social et environnemental du secteur
-  Préparer l’avenir à travers des projets durables et adaptés

Avec plus de 84 600 logements gérés, les sociétés membres de l’ARMOS-oi logent plus d’un Réunionnais sur quatre et emploient environ 1 400 salariés.
Aménageurs, constructeurs et logeurs, les opérateurs de l’ARMOS-oi réalisent chaque année plusieurs milliers de nouveaux logements et pilotent la majorité des opérations d’aménagement public de l’île.

Name: Clare Housing
Description:
Clare Housing’s mission is to provide a continuum of affordable and supportive housing options that create healing communities and optimize the health of people living with HIV/AIDS.

Inequity in housing access and housing instability are linked with HIV: People living with HIV experience a higher probability of housing loss and instability due to the disabling and impoverishing effects of HIV. Similarly, the experience of homelessness increases the risk of HIV infection. People experiencing homelessness have HIV rates as much as 16 times higher than those who are stably housed. 

There is no cure for HIV, but medications if taken daily can reduce the virus to the point where it is no longer transmittable. Getting and keeping an undetectable viral load is the best way for people living with HIV to stay healthy and protect others. 

We opened our first community care home in 1987 for individuals needing end-of-life care resulting from HIV/AIDS. Today, we are the largest provider of HIV-specific housing in Minnesota, serving about 300 individuals each year. We house and serve people who are usually excluded from other housing options. 

By providing safe, low-barrier supportive housing, we have helped thousands of people living with HIV to find a sense of belonging and community and take steps towards wholeness.  So many of our Clare Housing residents have told us they’ve been able to get sober, or reconnect with family, or volunteer or get a job, or start taking essential medications—because of the stability they now have.

Supportive housing is also good for our entire community. Among countless other benefits, supportive housing saves money by reducing the need for expensive emergency medical care and other crisis services.

Learn more by attending a virtual HIV & Homelessness 101 session. https://www.clarehousing.org/get-involved/hiv101/

Name: LIFE BE-WoodEN
Description:
LIFE Be-WoodEN (LIFE23-PRE-IT-LIFE BE-WoodEN)-is a project co-funded by the European Commission in the framework of the LIFE program.
The Project Consortium is led by the University of Genoa (UNIGE - Italy) and includes the following members: Liguria Region (RL - Italy), ART-ER Società Consortile per Azioni (ARTER - Italy) - University of Florence (UNIFI - Italy) - FederlegnoArredo (FLA - Italy) - Cecodhas Housing Europe (EU Association) - Wroclaw University of Science and Technology (PWR - Poland) - University of Primorska (UP - Slovenia)
The project started in February 2024 and its development will last until January 2026.

PROJECT FRAMEWORK
Sustainability, Beauty, Inclusion: the cornerstone principles of the New European Bauhaus initiative, inspired the design concept of LIFE Be-WoodEN. 

LIFE Be-WoodEN project aims to:
- make buildings more environmentally friendly by promoting the use of wood and bio-based materials; 
- overcome cultural and technical barriers and economic challenges that currently hinder the widespread adoption of wood and bio-based materials in construction; 
- address NEB principles along the whole process: from production and design to installation, facility management, dismantling. 
It focuses on: 
- increasing professionals’ knowledge and skills, by applying innovative training methods, such as webinars, podcasts, innovation laboratories, co-design approaches, Challenge Based Learning activities; 
- engaging a wide variety of stakeholders including professionals, public officers, companies, experts, and artists;
- testing innovative and sustainable building solutions, especially for social housing.

Funded by the European Union. Views and opinions expressed are however those of the author(s) only and do not necessarily reflect those of the European Union or of the European Climate, Infrastructure and Environment Executive Agency (CINEA). Neither the European Union nor the granting authority can be held responsible for them.

Name: Stiftelsen Anker
Description:
-	Stiftelsen Anker Studentboliger og Hotel (Anker STI) er en ideell stiftelse og har som hovedformål å tilby boliger til studenter og yrkesfagelever. Anker STI eier og forvalter 2 665 studentboliger innenfor ring 3 i Oslo.

-	Stiftelsen har en ambisjon om å tilby studentboliger billigere enn i det kommersielle markedet med god kvalitet og studentvennlige leiekontrakter. I dagens pressede leiemarked er stiftelsens leiepriser 20-30% under markedsleie i Oslo.

-	Anker STI driver overnattingsvirksomhet og utleie av kommersielle forretningsarealer som subsidierer det samfunnsnyttige formålet og gjør driften av studentboliger økonomisk forsvarlig. Anker STI mottar per i dag ikke statsstøtte eller husbanklån til nye prosjekter. Stiftelsen betaler skatt, formuesskatt og eiendomsskatt.

-      Stiftelsen ble etablert på 1970-tallet av Oslo Kommune, NHO og LO. De opprinnelige stifterne utnevner flertallet i stiftelsens representantskap. Representantskapet velger styrets medlemmer.

Description: Canmore Community Housing (CCH) is a non-profit corporation wholly owned by the Town of Canmore and is operated as an independent organization. We achieve this through research, education, advocacy and our Vital Homes (previously called Perpetually Affordable Housing), Accessory Dwelling Grants, and Matching Down Deposit programs.

Name: Worldwide Employee Housing Solutions
Description:
At Worldwide Employee Housing Solutions, we specialize in providing comfortable, affordable, and convenient housing solutions to businesses across the USA and beyond. Our factory-built homes are designed to meet the unique needs of businesses in a variety of industries, and our team of experts handles everything from setup and installation to maintenance and repairs.
We understand the challenges that businesses face when it comes to housing their temporary employees. Traditional housing options can be expensive, inconvenient, and lack the amenities that employees need to feel comfortable and productive. 
Our mission is to help businesses attract and retain employees by providing the best possible housing solutions. We are dedicated to delivering personalized service, quality craftsmanship, and exceptional value to each and every one of our clients. If you're looking for a temporary employee housing solution that is affordable, convenient, and comfortable, look no further than Worldwide Employee Housing Solutions.

Name: Ocean's Harbor House
Description: Ocean’s Harbor House is committed to its core mission of serving vulnerable populations by providing housing and essential supports to young people through The SHORE House (Supportive Housing Opportunities through Restoration and Encouragement) Residential Program, Our Out-Client Day Services, our Intensive Street Outreach, and comprehensive Aftercare.

Name: Chicago Housing Trust

Name: Yesterday’s Gone
Description: Yesterday’s Gone believes that no woman should have to go back to an abusive situation. Our program assists women who have suffered abuse or trauma by offering housing, life coach services, and access to local resources. Yesterday’s Gone guides them on a healing journey towards self-sustainability. Our Vision is to live in a world where no woman has to go back to an abusive situation.

Name: JEUNESSE HABITAT SOLIDAIRE
